w. A. Coplen, superintendent OF
the mining department Of ths inter-
state fair, which will Be held IN this
city October 3-s, is In Stevens county
this week looking up the mine owners
with view OF securing exhibits for
the fair. Last year q number Of ex-
hibits were sent in from this section
but 1t il the desire Of the manage
ment to greatly increase the number
and quality of these exhibits. AL
Of the displays which were at the
fair last year have been preserved
and will be on exhibition this year.
The mining interests Of Stevens coun-
ty have never been properly repre-
scented. 1t In the intention Of the
fair management to secure samples
from all the prominent mines and
from recent strikes in the district.


The mining department will make
awards this year for the best mineral
exhibits. The exact nature Of the
prizes has not been decided. but will
be Of q nature to encourage competi-
tion. Three judges win award the
prizes. Two have already been
chosen. They are c. NJ. Fassett, ths
assayer, and L. X. Armstrons. thu
editor Of Mining. The third Judge
will be selected later.
